title: Data space inversion for efficient predictions and uncertainty quantification for geothermal models

authors.name: Alex de Beer
authors.affiliation: Department of Engineering Science and Biomedical Engineering, University of Auckland, Auckland 1010, New Zealand

method.name: Data space inversion (DSI)
method.description: An efficient method for making model predictions with quantified uncertainty in a geothermal context
key_features: Circumvents the calibration process by directly estimating predictive quantities conditioned on measured data
Does not require access to model derivatives
Can be applied to non-differentiable models
Computation can be carried out in parallel

key_points: DSI is an efficient method for making model predictions with quantified uncertainty in a geothermal context
Provides a thorough evaluation of the DSI framework using two model problems in geothermal reservoir engineering
DSI can produce accurate predictions at a significantly lower computational cost than conventional methods
applications: Previously applied in subsurface hydrology and petroleum engineering

comparison_with_other_methods.difference: Previous BEL application to geothermal modeling considered only single-phase, natural state simulations
